licensing.board: Alabama Licensing Board for General Contractors
requirements: Projects over $50,000 require license
Pass trade exam and business law exam
Proof of insurance required
Financial statement required for projects over $150,000

lien.preliminary_notice: Not required for prime contractors
lien.deadline: Within 6 months of last furnishing labor/materials

taxes.contractor_tax: Use tax may apply on materials

licensing.board: Alaska Department of Commerce, Community, and Economic Development
requirements: General Contractor Residential: projects up to $25,000
General Contractor Commercial: projects over $25,000
Surety bond required ($5,000 - $25,000)
Insurance required

lien.preliminary_notice: Not required
lien.deadline: Within 90 days of last furnishing labor/materials

taxes.sales_tax: No state sales tax (local taxes may apply)
taxes.contractor_tax: Contractor tax applies to certain projects

licensing.board: Arizona Registrar of Contractors
requirements: License required for projects over $1,000
Pass trade exam and business management exam
Bond required ($4,250 - $15,000 based on classification)
Workers' compensation insurance required

lien.preliminary_notice: Required within 20 days of first furnishing

taxes.contractor_tax: Prime contractors exempt from sales tax on materials

CA.name: California

licensing.board: California Contractors State License Board (CSLB)
requirements: License required for projects $1,000 or more (raised from $500 by AB 2622, effective Jan 1, 2025 — first change since 1939)
4 years of journeyman-level experience required (Bus. & Prof. Code §7068)
Pass Law and Business exam + trade exam for your classification
$25,000 contractor bond required (raised from $15,000 by SB 607, effective Jan 1, 2023)
Workers' compensation required if you have employees; C-20 HVAC must carry regardless (universal mandate punted to Jan 1, 2028 by SB 1455)
Common classifications: B (General Building), C-10 (Electrical), C-20 (HVAC/Mechanical), C-36 (Plumbing), C-51 (Structural Steel)

lien.preliminary_notice: 20-Day Preliminary Notice required for subs/suppliers (Civ. Code §8200). Late service forfeits lien rights for work performed before the 20-day lookback.
lien.deadline: Lien must be recorded within 90 days of completion, or 60 days after owner records Notice of Completion/Cessation (Civ. Code §8412, §8414)
lien.foreclosure: Suit to foreclose must commence within 90 days of recording the lien (Civ. Code §8460). Hard expiration by operation of law.

taxes.sales_tax: 7.25% state base + local district taxes up to ~3.5% (combined max ~10.75%)
taxes.contractor_tax: Materials: contractor is consumer (pays tax at purchase). Fixtures: contractor is retailer (collects tax from customer). See CDTFA Reg. 1521 — most-litigated contractor tax issue in California.

notes_2026: SB 61 (eff. Jan 1, 2026) — Private-works retention capped at 5% by statute (new Civ. Code §8811). Owners previously withheld 10-15% with no cap.

SB 440 (eff. Jan 1, 2026) — Change-order claims procedure: mandatory 30-day owner response, 10-business-day post-meet-and-confer confirmation, 60-day payment of undisputed amounts, non-waivable right to suspend work for nonpayment.
Workers' comp universal mandate moved from Jan 1, 2026 to Jan 1, 2028 (SB 1455).

TX.name: Texas

licensing.board: Trade-specific (no statewide GC license): TDLR for HVAC/Mechanical & Electrical; Texas State Board of Plumbing Examiners (TSBPE) for Plumbing; no state license for General Contractor or Structural Steel.
requirements: General Contractor: NO state license required; local registration in major metros (Houston, Dallas, San Antonio, Austin) — verify with city building department
Mechanical / HVAC: TDLR-licensed (Air Conditioning & Refrigeration Contractor) under Tex. Occ. Code Chapter 1302
Electrical: TDLR-licensed under Tex. Occ. Code Chapter 1305 — Master, Journeyman, Residential Wireman classifications
Plumbing: separate TSBPE licensure under Tex. Occ. Code Chapter 1301 — Master Plumber (now 2-year journeyman experience, reduced from 4 by SB 947 eff. Sep 1, 2025)
Structural Steel: NO state license; AISC/AWS certifications + OSHA training apply
Workers' comp: Texas is the only state that allows opt-out (non-subscriber). State public works require WC under Tex. Lab. Code §406.096.

lien.preliminary_notice: Monthly Pre-Lien Notice: residential = 15th day of 2nd month after unpaid work; commercial = 15th day of 3rd month. Each unpaid month requires its own notice. HB 2237 (2022) eliminated USPS Registered Mail — Certified Mail or traceable private courier only under Tex. Prop. Code §53.003.
lien.deadline: Lien Affidavit recording: residential = 15th day of 3rd month after work ended; commercial = 15th day of 4th month (Tex. Prop. Code §53.052).
lien.foreclosure: Suit to foreclose: 1 year after recording for residential, 2 years for commercial (Tex. Prop. Code §53.158).

taxes.sales_tax: 6.25% state base + local up to 2% (combined max 8.25%) — no state income tax
taxes.contractor_tax: Lump-sum contract: contractor is consumer of incorporated materials (pays tax at purchase). Separated contract: contractor charges sales tax on materials portion. Commercial repair/remodel LABOR is sales-taxable in TX — Texas Comptroller Rule 3.291.

notes_2026: Texas Construction Trust Fund Act (Tex. Prop. Code Ch. 162): every construction payment received is a trust fund. Diverting $500+ without paying subs = Class A misdemeanor; 3rd-degree felony (2-10 yrs prison) with intent to defraud. Corporate veil does NOT shield the officer.
Retainage: 10% statutory minimum on commercial private projects (Tex. Prop. Code §53.101) — TX has no statewide private-works retainage CAP, only a floor.

2025 89th Legislature changes: SB 929 (weekend/holiday deadline extension), SB 947 (Master Plumber experience 4→2 yrs), HB 2960 (voids out-of-state venue clauses), HB 3005 (ends perpetual-audit payment delay on public works).
Homestead lien trap: liens on residential homesteads require pre-work written contracts signed by both spouses and filed with the county clerk BEFORE any work begins.

FL.name: Florida

licensing.board: Department of Business and Professional Regulation (DBPR), Construction Industry Licensing Board (CILB)
requirements: Statutory authority: Florida Statutes Chapter 489. No minimum project-value threshold like CA/TN.

CERTIFIED vs REGISTERED: Certified contractors work statewide. Registered contractors can ONLY work in jurisdictions where they registered. A registered contractor working outside their county = Class A misdemeanor, escalating to 3rd-degree FELONY during any declared state of emergency (§ 489.127(2)(c)).

Classifications: Class A General Contractor (unlimited), Class B Building Contractor, Class C Residential. Trade classes: Class A Air Conditioning (mechanical), Class B HVAC (limited tonnage), Class A Mechanical, electrical, plumbing.
Experience: 4 years documented at journeyman+ level (1 year supervisory) for primary qualifier
Financial responsibility: net worth + FICO ≥ 660 at application; below 660 requires 14-hour course + $10K-$20K bond by license class.
Workers' comp: required from the FIRST employee in construction (vs 4 employees minimum for non-construction industries). GC is statutory employer for any sub without WC.

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice to Owner (NTO) is the perennial Florida trap (Fla. Stat. § 713.06(2)(a)). Subs and suppliers MUST serve NTO before commencing work OR within 45 days of first furnishing. Day 46 = complete defense to lien.
lien.deadline: Claim of Lien recording: 90 days after last work or last materials (Fla. Stat. § 713.08). Final Contractor's Affidavit must be delivered to owner at least 5 days BEFORE filing suit.
lien.foreclosure: Suit to foreclose: 1 year after recording the lien (Fla. Stat. § 713.22). Owner can file a Notice of Contest of Lien to shorten the enforcement window to 60 days.

taxes.sales_tax: 6% state base + local discretionary sales surtax up to 1.5% (combined max ~7.5%). Fla. Stat. § 212.05.
taxes.contractor_tax: Real property improvement contractor = consumer of materials (pays sales tax at purchase). Tangible personal property installer = retailer. The lump-sum vs cost-plus vs time-and-materials structure changes the treatment. See FL Dept. of Revenue Rule 12A-1.051.

notes_2026: SB 658 (effective July 1, 2025) KILLED the "substantially similar" lien waiver safe harbor. Waiver and release forms must match Fla. Stat. § 713.20 statutory templates EXACTLY.
SB 346 (effective July 1, 2023) flattened public-works retainage to 5% throughout the project. Punch list within 30 days of substantial completion.
SB 360 (effective April 13, 2023) cut the construction-defect statute of repose from 10 to 7 years. One-year grace period expired July 1, 2024.
Misapplication of construction funds (Fla. Stat. § 713.345) is a FIRST-DEGREE FELONY at $100K or more (up to 30 years' prison). Officers personally criminally liable regardless of corporate form.
Construction during declared states of emergency requires extra care: unlicensed practice penalties escalate to felony tier.

NY.name: New York

licensing.board: Municipal patchwork — no state GC license. NYC: Dept. of Buildings (DOB) for GC Registration + Dept. of Consumer & Worker Protection (DCWP) for Home Improvement Contractor (residential). Nassau, Suffolk, Westchester, Rockland: separate county licensing.
requirements: NYC GC Registration (DOB) does NOT authorize work in Nassau — separate license. Treat every metro as its own jurisdiction.
NYC residential remodeling: BOTH NYC DOB GCR AND DCWP Home Improvement Contractor license are typically required
Master Plumber + Master Electrician: city-specific licenses (NYC has its own Master Plumber license under DOB)
Workers' comp required for all employers (NY WCL §2 et seq.); construction industry has no employee-count threshold
CE-200 workers' comp exemption covers ONLY the corporate officer personally — prime contractor still liable under WCL §56 for uncovered subs

lien.preliminary_notice: No statewide preliminary notice required. But Notice of Lending (Lien Law §13) and Notice of Furnishing (Lien Law §76 for trust-fund claims) are required for certain claim types.
lien.deadline: Mechanic's lien filing: 8 months from last work (commercial), 4 months (residential 1-2 family) (NY Lien Law §10). Public improvement: 30 days from completion or 90 days from acceptance (NY Lien Law §12).
lien.foreclosure: Suit to foreclose: 1 year after recording (NY Lien Law §17). Extendable by court order or extension filed before expiry.

taxes.sales_tax: 4% state + local up to 4.875% (NYC = 4.5%) — combined max ~8.875% (NY Tax Law §1105)
taxes.contractor_tax: Capital improvement to real property: NOT taxable to customer (contractor pays tax on materials at purchase). Repair/maintenance: TAXABLE to customer. Form ST-124 (Certificate of Capital Improvement) must be obtained from customer BEFORE work begins or the DTF can re-characterize as repair and assess sales tax on the entire contract.

notes_2026: Pay-if-paid clauses are VOID in NY (West-Fair Electric Contractors v. Aetna Cas. & Sur. Co., 87 N.Y.2d 148, 1995).

Article 3-A trust fund liability (NY Lien Law §70 et seq.): every construction payment is a statutory trust fund. Diverting trust funds = larceny under Lien Law §79-a, scaling to Class B felony (up to 25 years) for diversions over $1M. NO corporate-veil protection.
HIC non-compliance forfeits BOTH contract enforcement AND lien rights against the homeowner (NY Gen. Bus. Law §771 series).
5% retainage cap on private commercial applies ONLY to contracts signed on or after November 17, 2023 AND for contract amounts > $150,000 (NY Gen. Bus. Law §756-c, effective 2023).
NYC Local Law 196 (2017) — Site Safety Training cards required for construction workers; 40 hours of SST minimum.
NYC Local Law 152 (2016) — Periodic gas piping inspection by licensed Master Plumber every 4 years.

IL.name: Illinois

licensing.board: Illinois has NO statewide General Contractor license. Trade-specific state licensing: Plumbing under Illinois Department of Public Health (225 ILCS 320). Roofing under Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR, 225 ILCS 335). Electrical and HVAC are licensed MUNICIPALLY.
requirements: Plumbing: Illinois state license required (225 ILCS 320); $20,000 bond.
Roofing: Illinois state license required (225 ILCS 335) — $10,000 limited bond OR $25,000 unlimited.
Electrical/HVAC: NO statewide license; each municipality licenses separately. A Chicago Master Electrician license does NOT authorize work in Aurora.
General Contractor: no state license; major cities require local registration (Chicago: GC license Classes A-E with insurance limits scaling from $1M GL for Class E up to $5M GL for Class A).
Workers' comp (820 ILCS 305): NO employee-count threshold for construction. Auto-applies from one worker up.

Worker classification trap: 820 ILCS 185 (Employee Classification Act) presumes construction workers are EMPLOYEES unless contractor proves all 3 prongs of the ABC test. Penalties: $1K-$2.5K per worker + doubled damages + personal officer liability.

lien.preliminary_notice: 90-day Subcontractor Notice (770 ILCS 60/24): must be served on owner AND lender within 90 days of last work. As of January 2025 (Public Act 103-0827), FedEx/UPS/DHL with tracking are now accepted in addition to certified mail — and the legal service date is the MAILING date, not delivery.
lien.deadline: Original contractor lien: 4 months from completion to file with county recorder AND preserve lien rights against bona-fide purchasers and lenders. After 4 months: rights survive ONLY against the original owner.
lien.foreclosure: Suit to foreclose: must commence within 2 years of completion. But Section 34 demand (770 ILCS 60/34) — the owner can serve a written demand and force you to sue within 30 days or forfeit lien rights entirely.

taxes.sales_tax: 6.25% state base + local up to 4.75% (combined max ~11% in Cook County / Chicago). 35 ILCS 120/2.
taxes.contractor_tax: Real property improvement contractor = consumer of materials. Chicago Home Rule adds 1.25% beyond local. Manufacturing facility exemptions exist.

notes_2026: Public Act 103-0827 (eff. January 1, 2025) modernized 770 ILCS 60/24: traceable couriers (FedEx/UPS/DHL with tracking) are now valid for sub notices, and date of MAILING — not delivery — is the legal service date.

Retainage cap: 10% initial, drops to 5% at 50% complete. Applies to private (since 2019), local government (since January 2024 — Public Act 103-0445), state agencies (effective June 2027). Residential 12-units-or-fewer exempt.
ILLINOIS HAS NO CONSTRUCTION TRUST FUND like Texas Ch. 162 or NY Article 3-A.
Chicago Class A-E GC license tiers scale required insurance from $1M GL (Class E) to $5M GL (Class A).
Section 34 demand procedure (770 ILCS 60/34) is unique — the owner can shrink your foreclosure window to 30 days.

GA.name: Georgia

licensing.board: Georgia State Licensing Board for Residential and General Contractors (SLBRGC) under Georgia Secretary of State. Trade-specific licensing (Electrical, Plumbing, HVAC, Low-Voltage) is separate under O.C.G.A. Title 43 Chapter 14.
requirements: Project value thresholds with REAL teeth: Residential Basic Contractor required for residential projects $2,500+; General Contractor required for commercial $100,000+.

O.C.G.A. § 43-41-17(b): a contract signed by an unlicensed contractor above the threshold is UNENFORCEABLE — no lawsuit, no lien, no quantum meruit. Getting licensed later does NOT cure prior contracts.
Experience: 2 years documented for Residential-Basic; 4 years for General Contractor unlimited tier
Workers' comp required for employers with 3+ employees; statutory employer rule (O.C.G.A. § 34-9-8) means GC's WC policy backstops any sub without coverage.
HOLDING THE GC LICENSE DOES NOT AUTHORIZE TRADE WORK. Self-performing electrical, plumbing, HVAC, or low-voltage requires separate Title 43 Chapter 14 licenses.

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice to Contractor (NTC) required by sub-subs and suppliers NOT in direct privity with the prime contractor. 30-day fuse from Notice of Commencement filing OR first delivery (O.C.G.A. § 44-14-361.5). Miss the 30-day deadline = lien rights die entirely.
lien.deadline: Claim of Lien recording: 90 days from last day of furnishing labor or material (O.C.G.A. § 44-14-361.1). 2-BUSINESS-DAY service deadline: copy of the recorded lien must be served on the owner within 2 business days of filing — miss it and the lien is VOID.
lien.foreclosure: Suit to enforce lien must commence within 365 days of filing the lien (O.C.G.A. § 44-14-361.1(a)(3)).

taxes.sales_tax: 4% state base + local up to 5% (combined max 9%) — O.C.G.A. § 48-8-30 series
taxes.contractor_tax: Real-property improvement contractor = consumer of materials (pays sales tax at purchase). Manufacturing facility exemptions exist for specific projects.

notes_2026: SB 143 (2021) — Interim lien waivers (O.C.G.A. § 44-14-366) convert to UNCONDITIONAL after 90 days. If you sign an interim waiver against a check that bounces or short-pays, you MUST file an Affidavit of Nonpayment in superior court within 90 days.
Prompt Payment Act (O.C.G.A. § 13-11-1 et seq.) interest is 1%/month — BUT § 13-11-7 only attaches the interest if the pay application STATES the statutory notice.
Public works retainage typically 10%, reducible at 50% complete per contract.

NC.name: North Carolina

licensing.board: North Carolina Licensing Board for General Contractors (NCLBGC). Trade-specific licensing under separate boards: NC Board of Examiners of Electrical Contractors, NC State Board of Examiners of Plumbing, Heating and Fire Sprinkler Contractors.
requirements: Project value threshold: $30,000+ requires license (N.C. Gen. Stat. § 87-1)

GC monetary classifications are PER-PROJECT caps, NOT annual: Limited ($750,000), Intermediate ($1,500,000), Unlimited (no cap). A Limited GC cannot lawfully take a $900K project even if annual volume stays under $1M — operating outside classification VOIDS the entire contract under Brady v. Fulghum.
Working capital: $17,000 Limited / $80,000 Intermediate / $150,000 Unlimited
Experience: 1-3 years documented (escalates with classification tier)
Holding NCLBGC GC license does NOT authorize self-performing electrical, plumbing, HVAC, or fire-sprinkler work.
Workers' comp required for 3+ employees, BUT § 97-19 statutory-employer liability kicks in if cumulative on-site headcount (GC + uninsured sub's employees) reaches 3.

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice to Lien Agent via LiensNC.com — for any project $30,000+, owner must register the project with a Lien Agent (NC Gen. Stat. § 44A-11.1). Every sub and supplier must serve Notice to Lien Agent through LiensNC.com WITHIN 15 CALENDAR DAYS of first furnishing. Day 16+ = lien rights for pre-notice work are FORFEIT.
lien.deadline: Claim of Lien on Real Property: 120 days from last furnishing (N.C. Gen. Stat. § 44A-12). Lien priority relates back to PROJECT first furnishing (not claimant first furnishing).
lien.foreclosure: Suit to enforce: 180 days from last furnishing (NC Gen. Stat. § 44A-13).

taxes.sales_tax: 4.75% state base + local up to 2.75% (combined max 7.5%). N.C. Gen. Stat. § 105-164.4.
taxes.contractor_tax: 2017 sales tax overhaul created TWO regimes. (1) Real Property Contract (RPC): new construction → contractor is CONSUMER of materials. (2) Repair, Maintenance, Installation Services (RMI): service-call work → contractor is RETAILER, collects sales tax on FULL GROSS RECEIPTS.

notes_2026: Pre-furnishing lien waivers are VOID under N.C. Gen. Stat. § 44A-12.1(b) — despite aggressive subcontract boilerplate from some GCs.
LiensNC.com / Lien Agent system (effective April 2013 for $30K+ projects) has matured — the 15-day Notice to Lien Agent service window is the single most-litigated deadline.
§ 97-19 statutory-employer rule extends beyond the 3-employee floor when cumulative project headcount crosses 3.
Brady v. Fulghum doctrine: a contractor operating beyond their monetary classification CANNOT enforce the contract OR a quantum meruit claim.

WA.name: Washington

licensing.board: Washington Department of Labor & Industries (L&I) — Contractor Registration (RCW 18.27, NOT licensure). Trade-specific licensing under separate state regulators: Electrical (RCW 19.28), Plumbing (RCW 18.106).
requirements: Registration (not licensing) under RCW 18.27 — Washington uses a financial-filing model rather than competency exam for GCs. Two classes: General Contractor and Specialty Contractor.
Bond requirements DOUBLED effective July 1, 2024 (HB 1534): $30,000 for General, $15,000 for Specialty (up from $12K / $6K).
Insurance: $200,000 public liability + $50,000 property damage MINIMUMS (RCW 18.27.050)
Workers' comp: MONOPOLY state — only Washington L&I State Fund or qualified self-insurance.
Electrical (RCW 19.28) and Plumbing (RCW 18.106) are EXAM-based licenses, separate from L&I registration.
RCW 18.27.080 SUIT BAR: any registration lapse DURING work strips collection rights.

lien.preliminary_notice: Pre-Claim Notice (Notice of Right to Claim Lien) required for residential 1-4 unit dwellings — 10-day lookback for new SFR construction, 60-day lookback for commercial (RCW 60.04.031). Among the shortest in the country.
lien.deadline: Claim of Lien: 90 days from last work (RCW 60.04.091). Plus a 14-day owner-service deadline AFTER recording — miss it and you forfeit attorney's fees.
lien.foreclosure: Suit to foreclose: 8 months from filing (RCW 60.04.141). Foreclosure summons must be served within 90 days of filing. Residential remodel liens are capped at unpaid-to-prime amounts (RCW 60.04.031(3)).

taxes.sales_tax: 6.5% state base + local up to 4% (combined max 10.6% in some jurisdictions). RCW 82.08.020.
taxes.contractor_tax: WASHINGTON IS THE INVERSE OF MOST STATES: construction LABOR is a retail sale under RCW 82.04.050(2)(b). Contractor charges sales tax on the TOTAL CONTRACT (labor + materials + markup). Plus: B&O (Business & Occupation) tax on GROSS RECEIPTS — 0.471% Retailing / 0.484% Wholesaling, no deductions.

notes_2026: Bond requirements DOUBLED effective July 1, 2024 (HB 1534): General Contractor bond $30,000 (was $12K), Specialty Contractor $15,000 (was $6K).
Homeowner Recovery Program SURCHARGE begins July 1, 2026 — new fee on contractor registrations.
Sales-tax-on-total-contract is the single biggest WA gotcha. Out-of-state contractors face 4-YEAR audit recovery on the full labor-component tax delta.
Monopoly WC (Ohio is the other monopoly state). Construction rates are among the highest in the country.

licensing.board: No state-level licensing (local jurisdictions)
requirements: No state-level general contractor license
Denver, Boulder, and other cities require local licenses
Plumbing and electrical state-licensed
General liability insurance typically required locally
Workers' compensation required if employees

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Intent to Lien required at least 10 days before filing lien
lien.deadline: Within 4 months (residential) or 6 months (commercial) of last furnishing
lien.foreclosure: Within 6 months of project completion or last furnishing

taxes.contractor_tax: Materials taxable; labor exempt for new construction

OH.name: Ohio

licensing.board: NO statewide General Contractor license — licensing is municipal. State-licensed trades under Ohio Construction Industry Licensing Board (OCILB): HVAC, Refrigeration, Electrical, Plumbing, Hydronics (ORC Chapter 4740). Workers' comp MONOPOLY through Ohio Bureau of Workers' Compensation.
requirements: Working in 5 Ohio cities = 5 separate municipal registrations. Columbus ($25K bond + $300/yr), Cleveland ($150 + $120 renewal), Cincinnati ($131.25), Dayton ($10K trade bond), Toledo, Akron.
OCILB trade licenses: Master and Contractor tiers, exam + experience required
AS OF JAN 1, 2025: OCILB electrical contractors need a SEPARATE $120 annual registration ON TOP OF the underlying state license.
Workers' comp: Ohio BWC MONOPOLY. NO private market — Travelers/Hartford/Liberty DO NOT write WC in Ohio.
Municipal income tax: every Ohio city you work in wants its own income tax return.

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Furnishing (NOF) required by 2nd-tier subs and suppliers — must be served on owner/owner's designee within 21 CALENDAR DAYS of first furnishing (ORC § 1311.05). Late NOF = lien rights for work performed more than 21 days BEFORE service are FORFEIT permanently.
lien.deadline: THREE DIFFERENT CLOCKS based on project type: 60 days from last work for 1-2 family residential; 75 days for commercial/multi-family; 120 days for oil/gas projects (ORC § 1311.06).
lien.foreclosure: Standard 6 years for written contracts under ORC § 2305.06, but lien-specific procedures via § 1311.11.

taxes.sales_tax: 5.75% state base + local up to 2.25% (combined max 8%). ORC Chapter 5739.
taxes.contractor_tax: Contractor as consumer of materials for real-property construction. Commercial Activity Tax (CAT) on gross receipts also applies — separate from sales tax.

notes_2026: HB 50 (effective September 20, 2024) — ALL residential remodels $25,000+ now governed by Home Construction Service Suppliers Act (HCSSA). Written-contract requirements, 3-day right of rescission.
OCILB electrical contractor SEPARATE $120 annual registration effective January 1, 2025.
Ohio Prompt Pay Act (ORC § 4113.61): unpaid subs collect 18% INTEREST + attorney fees when a prime sits on owner-received funds more than 10 days.
Ohio BWC monopoly is one of only TWO states with this structure (Washington is the other).

PA.name: Pennsylvania

licensing.board: PA has NO statewide General Contractor or trade license. Home Improvement Contractor (HIC) registration is statewide under PA Attorney General (HICPA — 73 P.S. § 517.1 et seq.). GC and trade licensing is MUNICIPAL.
requirements: HICPA registration TRIGGER: $5,000+ ANNUAL revenue from residential remodeling work. $50/year fee.
HICPA written-contract TRIGGER: $500+ PER CONTRACT — different from the registration trigger.
HICPA DOWN-PAYMENT CAP: 1/3 of contract OR $1,000, whichever is GREATER. Industry-standard 30-50% deposits are ILLEGAL.
HICPA fraud at $2,000+ advance payment is a 3rd-DEGREE FELONY. If consumer is age 60+, bumps to 2nd-DEGREE FELONY (10 years/$25K).
Municipal licensing patchwork: Philadelphia eCLIPSE, Pittsburgh PLI, Allegheny County for plumbing. None reciprocal.
CWMA (Construction Workplace Misclassification Act, 43 P.S. § 933.1) has TEETH: three-part test for 1099 status, $1K-$2.5K per-worker civil penalties.

lien.preliminary_notice: Subs MUST serve a 30-day Preliminary Notice of Intent to Lien before filing (no exceptions). Act 142 added a SCND Notice of Furnishing for projects > $1.5M — 45-day window from first furnishing.
lien.deadline: Filing: 6 months from completion for primes; 4 months for subs (49 P.S. § 1502).
lien.foreclosure: Suit to enforce: 2 years from filing (49 P.S. § 1701 series). Owner-occupied residential FULL-PAYMENT DEFENSE (Act 117) extinguishes sub liens.

taxes.contractor_tax: Contractor as consumer of materials for real-property improvements.

notes_2026: CASPA (Contractor and Subcontractor Payment Act, 73 P.S. § 501 et seq.) is THE MOST AGGRESSIVE prompt-pay statute in the country: 1% interest + 1% penalty = 24% ANNUALIZED, NON-WAIVABLE post-2018.
HICPA down-payment cap of 1/3 or $1,000 is the trap that catches the most out-of-state remodelers.
HICPA fraud felony grading is among the harshest in the US.
Pittsburgh-area plumbing is licensed by Allegheny COUNTY Health Department, NOT the city.
CWMA three-part test: (1) written contract, (2) freedom from direction and control, (3) independently engaged trade with $50K GL.

MI.name: Michigan

licensing.board: Michigan LARA (Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs). Residential Builder + Maintenance & Alteration Contractor (MCL Ch. 339). Commercial GC: NO statewide license — Stille-DeRossett-Hale Act delegates to municipalities.
requirements: Residential Builder license TRIGGER: $600 — extraordinarily low compared to peer states.
Maintenance and Alteration Contractor license: separate specialty classifications (carpentry, concrete, etc.)
Commercial work: no statewide license; municipal patchwork — Detroit, Grand Rapids, Lansing, Ann Arbor each register separately.
Trade licensing under state regulators: Electrical, Plumbing, Mechanical/HVAC — all recodified to MCL Chapter 339 in 2018.
Workers' comp construction TRIGGER is 1 EMPLOYEE under MCL 418.151(2), not 3. Plus statutory-employer doctrine (MCL 418.171).

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Furnishing required by subs and suppliers within 20 CALENDAR DAYS of first furnishing (MCL 570.1109). One day SHORTER than Ohio's 21-day window — multi-state trap. Notice of Commencement required for projects > $30,000.
lien.deadline: Filing deadline: 90 days from last day of furnishing (MCL 570.1111). Sworn Statement from GC must list all subs and suppliers paid and owed; knowingly false = misdemeanor.
lien.foreclosure: Suit to enforce: 1 year from filing (MCL 570.1117).

taxes.sales_tax: 6% state base — NO LOCAL SALES TAX. Uniform statewide.
taxes.contractor_tax: Contractor as consumer of materials for real-property construction (standard model). MCL 205.52.

notes_2026: MICHIGAN BUILDERS TRUST FUND ACT (MCL 570.151 et seq.): every owner payment is a statutory TRUST FUND. Diverting funds before paying subs is a FELONY — up to 3 YEARS PRISON. Pierces the LLC veil — officers personally criminally liable. Pay-if-paid is NOT a defense.
Statutory employer doctrine (MCL 418.171) makes the GC DIRECTLY LIABLE for an uninsured sub's injured workers.
$600 Residential Builder threshold is the LOWEST in the country among states audited.
20-day Notice of Furnishing vs Ohio's 21-day NOF — one day off. Contractors crossing state lines miss this.
Prevailing Wage Act of 2023 was REPEALED in 2018 then RESTORED effective Feb 13, 2024 — applies only to state-funded construction.

VA.name: Virginia

licensing.board: Virginia Department of Professional and Occupational Regulation (DPOR), Board for Contractors.
requirements: License classifications are DUAL-THRESHOLD (per-project AND annual aggregate): Class A (unlimited), Class B ($120K-$750K per project / $750K annual cap), Class C ($1,000-$10,000 per project / $150K annual cap).
Class C trap: 40 jobs at $5,000 each = $200,000 annual aggregate — blows the $150K Class C cap. § 54.1-1115 treats this as UNLICENSED practice.
Specialty classifications LAYER on top: Class A RBC (Residential Building) does NOT authorize commercial work.
NEW April 1, 2025: Residential HVAC Mechanic and Residential Plumber tier — RESIDENTIAL-ONLY licenses; operating on a commercial job is a misdemeanor.
Workers' comp required for 3+ employees BUT § 65.2-302 statutory-employer doctrine kicks in below that threshold for construction.
QI / DE separation triggers a 60-day clock to designate a replacement or the firm's license SUSPENDS.

lien.preliminary_notice: General contractors have automatic lien rights without preliminary notice. Subs and material suppliers must follow specific notice procedures per Va. Code § 43-7 et seq.
lien.deadline: Memorandum of Mechanic's Lien: 90 days from the last day of the MONTH in which work was performed — NOT 90 days from last day of work (Va. Code § 43-4). 150-DAY LOOK-BACK: any labor more than 150 days before your last day of work VOIDS the entire memorandum.
lien.foreclosure: Suit to enforce: 6 months from recording or 60 days from completion of project, whichever is later (Va. Code § 43-17).

taxes.sales_tax: 4.3% state + 1% local (combined base 5.3%). Northern Virginia and Hampton Roads regions add an EXTRA 0.7%. Historic Triangle hits 7.0%.
taxes.contractor_tax: Contractor as consumer of materials. PLUS the BPOL TAX (Business, Professional, and Occupational License) — gross-receipts tax assessed by every Virginia locality where you work.

notes_2026: SB 550 (effective January 1, 2023) KILLED pay-if-paid clauses in Virginia. Primes owe subs within 60 days of submission of pay app regardless of owner payment status.
Class A/B/C dual-threshold trap is the single most-litigated contractor licensing issue in Virginia.
90-day-from-end-of-MONTH lien clock is unique to Virginia.
150-day look-back means a long job needs MULTIPLE successive lien filings.
BPOL tax in every working locality is easy to miss for out-of-state contractors.

MA.name: Massachusetts

licensing.board: Massachusetts dual licensing for residential: Home Improvement Contractor (HIC) registration with OCABR (M.G.L. c. 142A) PLUS Construction Supervisor License (CSL) issued by Department of Public Safety (M.G.L. c. 143 § 95). Trade: Electrical (c. 141), Plumbing/Gas (c. 142).
requirements: RESIDENTIAL DUAL-LICENSE TRAP: HIC registration (consumer protection regime) AND CSL (building-code regime) are INDEPENDENTLY required. Holding only one = exposed under both regimes simultaneously.
OWNER-PULLED PERMIT POISON PILL: contractor who lets the owner pull the permit FORFEITS Guaranty Fund eligibility under c. 142A § 5(c) AND is a PER SE 93A violation if used to evade CSL.
Workers' comp (M.G.L. c. 152): required for ALL employers regardless of size.
ABC test Prong B (independent contractor outside GC's usual business) torpedoes 1099 in core trades.
1/3 down-payment cap on residential remodels — anything more is VOIDABLE + 93A treble damages.

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Contract — prime contractor needs a WRITTEN contract with the owner. Notice of Subcontract — subs/suppliers must file to be in the lien fund.
lien.deadline: Multi-step front-loaded process (M.G.L. c. 254): Notice of Contract 90 days from last work for primes, Statement of Account 120 days, then enforcement suit within 90 days after Statement.
lien.foreclosure: Suit to enforce: 90 days after Statement of Account is recorded. SUB LIEN CAPPED at owner's unpaid balance to prime at the MOMENT of Notice of Subcontract filing.

taxes.sales_tax: 6.25% state — NO LOCAL sales tax. M.G.L. c. 64H.
taxes.contractor_tax: Contractor as consumer of materials. USE TAX on materials bought in NH (tax-free state) and brought into MA for installation is a SILENT KILLER — known DOR enforcement priority.

notes_2026: Chapter 93A (Mass consumer protection statute) is the per-se penalty machine. Almost any HIC technical violation = automatic TREBLE DAMAGES + attorney fees.
Net-Zero municipality patchwork: Brookline, Cambridge, Newton have PROHIBITED new fossil-fuel hookups in new construction.
MA Prompt Pay Act (c. 149 § 29E) only applies to PRIVATE projects $3,000,000+ — small/mid-sized residential work is not protected.
Dual residential licensing (HIC + CSL) is the most-litigated multi-state operator mistake.
Owner-pulled permit poison pill: if a contractor SUGGESTS the owner pull the permit, that suggestion alone is a per se 93A violation.

NJ.name: New Jersey

licensing.board: NJ Division of Consumer Affairs — Contractors' Registration Act (CRA, N.J.S.A. 56:8-136). Trade boards: NJ Board of Examiners of Electrical Contractors (N.J.S.A. 45:5A), NJ State Board of Master Plumbers (N.J.S.A. 45:14C), HVACR Contractor License (N.J.S.A. 45:16A).
requirements: Home Improvement Contractor (HIC) registration REQUIRED for any residential remodeling — N.J.S.A. 56:8-136. Annual $90 fee, $25,000 surety bond.
Trade licenses (Electrical, Plumbing, HVAC) are SEPARATE state licenses under their own boards.
PUBLIC WORKS Contractor Registration (N.J.S.A. 34:11-56.55) is a SEPARATE registration from CRA.
Workers' comp: required from the FIRST employee. No minimum. N.J.S.A. 34:15.
Personal officer liability under N.J.S.A. 34:15-79 for WC violations — pierces the LLC veil.
CIICA (Construction Industry Independent Contractor Act, N.J.S.A. 34:20-4): subs are PRESUMPTIVELY EMPLOYEES.

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Unpaid Balance and Right to File Lien (NUB) — required preliminary notice. For RESIDENTIAL 1-2 family owner-occupied: NUB must be filed within 60 days, THEN a Demand for AAA Arbitration within 60 more days, THEN binding arbitration award, THEN lien filing within 10 days. Commercial: NO arbitration prerequisite.
lien.deadline: Filing: 90 days from last work for commercial (N.J.S.A. 2A:44A-6). Residential: 60 days NUB, then arbitration chain.
lien.foreclosure: Suit to enforce: 1 year from filing (N.J.S.A. 2A:44A-14). LIEN FUND CAP: sub/supplier liens are LIMITED to the amount the owner still owes the prime (N.J.S.A. 2A:44A-9).

taxes.sales_tax: 6.625% state base. Urban Enterprise Zone (UEZ) reduced rate in specific cities.
taxes.contractor_tax: Capital improvement (Form ST-8) = NO sales tax on labor. Repair/maintenance/installation = labor IS sales-taxable. Misclassification = back tax + penalty.

notes_2026: CFA TREBLE-DAMAGES TRAP: any technical violation of the Contractors' Registration Act or N.J.A.C. 13:45A-16 is a PER SE Consumer Fraud Act violation — MANDATORY 3x damages + attorney fees (Cox v. Sears, Bosland v. Warnock). THE WORST CONSUMER-LITIGATION EXPOSURE IN THE COUNTRY.
Residential lien pre-arbitration requirement is unique to NJ.
Lien fund cap means joint checks become critical.
CIICA Prong B (work within GC's usual business) torpedoes nearly every construction 1099-sub arrangement.
Workers' comp from the FIRST employee — strictest among states audited.

TN.name: Tennessee

licensing.board: Tennessee Board for Licensing Contractors (TBLC)
requirements: License required before bidding or contracting on any project $25,000 or more (T.C.A. § 62-6-103)
Performing licensed work without a license = Class A misdemeanor + civil penalty up to $5,000 + disgorgement (§ 62-6-120)
Bond tiers: $500K bond if monetary limit < $1.5M; $1M bond if monetary limit ≥ $1.5M
Monetary limit = 10× working capital or net worth, whichever is less
Trade exam + Business and Law exam required
Financial statement: CPA-compiled now acceptable for renewal above $1.5M (TBLC rule change Nov 18, 2025); initial license still requires reviewed/audited at higher tiers
Common classifications: BC (General Building), CMC plus trade (Plumbing), CE (Electrical), S — Specialty Erection and Fabrication of Structural Steel
Home improvement contractors (residential remodels $3,000-$24,999): separate $10K bonded registration

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Nonpayment required by remote claimants — served monthly on owner, hard deadline 90 days from last day of month of work (T.C.A. § 66-11-145). Prime contractors have automatic lien rights, no notice required.
lien.deadline: Lien must be recorded within 90 days of completion or abandonment. If owner records Notice of Completion, the lien filing window shrinks to 10 days.
lien.foreclosure: Suit to enforce lien must commence within 1 year of recording (T.C.A. § 66-11-126).

taxes.sales_tax: 7% state base + local up to 2.75% (combined max ~9.75%)
taxes.contractor_tax: Contractor is consumer of materials (pays sales tax at purchase) for real-property contracts. Labor not separately taxed.

notes_2026: Retainage cap 5% on all projects, public and private (T.C.A. § 66-34-103). Prime contracts ≥ $500K must hold retainage in third-party interest-bearing escrow. Failure to escrow = damages up to $300/day.

Workers' comp construction-industry trap: T.C.A. § 50-6-902 has NO 5-employee threshold for construction. Coverage required at 1 employee including owners unless individually registered for exemption.
No 2025-2026 legislative changes to lien law or licensing thresholds. CIPPA (Public Chapter 605 of 2020) remains the operative retainage framework.

licensing.board: No state-level licensing (local jurisdictions)
requirements: No state-level general contractor license
Local jurisdictions set their own requirements
Indianapolis requires contractor license
Plumbing, HVAC, and electrical state-licensed
Insurance and bonding typically required locally

lien.preliminary_notice: Notice of Intent to File Lien may be sent
lien.deadline: Within 60 days (residential) or 90 days (commercial) of last furnishing

taxes.contractor_tax: Materials taxable; labor generally exempt

licensing.board: No state-level licensing (local jurisdictions)
requirements: No state-level general contractor license
Local jurisdictions set their own requirements
Kansas City and St. Louis require licenses
Electrical, plumbing, and HVAC state-licensed

Dwelling Contractor Certification required for permits
Must pass exam for Qualifier License

licensing.board: Connecticut Department of Consumer Protection

MD.name: Maryland

licensing.board: Maryland Home Improvement Commission (MHIC), under DLLR. Trade licenses: Electrical (Bus. Occ. § 6-301), Plumbing (Bus. Occ. § 12-301), HVAC (Bus. Reg. § 9A-101). Commercial: no state license (municipal — Baltimore, Montgomery County, etc.).
requirements: Home Improvement Contractor license required for any residential remodeling — Bus. Reg. § 8-101 et seq. Salesperson license separate from Contractor license.
BERENTER DOCTRINE: an unlicensed HIC contract is VOID — no lawsuit for unpaid balances, no quantum meruit. Homeowner keeps the work AND recovers any deposits paid.
Trade licensing separate (Electrical, Plumbing, HVAC) — each its own board.
Commercial GC: NO state license — Baltimore City/County, Montgomery, Prince George's, Howard, Anne Arundel each have separate registration regimes.
Workers' comp under Md. Code Lab. & Empl. § 9-202 — required for ALL employers (no minimum).
County local income tax stacks 2.25-3.20% on top of 5.75% state.

lien.preliminary_notice: Mechanics' liens in Maryland are JUDICIAL, not recorded — you PETITION the circuit court (Md. Real Prop. § 9-105, Md. Rule 12-302). Show-cause hearing within ~15 days; lien exists only by court order. 120-day subcontractor Notice of Intent to owner.
lien.deadline: 180-day petition deadline. 15% value-increase threshold for liens on existing buildings; tenant-occupied single-family dwellings are wholly excluded.
lien.foreclosure: Lien exists only by court order — once granted, foreclosure follows standard real-property procedure.

taxes.sales_tax: 6% state sales tax. County local income tax stacks 2.25-3.20% on top of 5.75% state — Baltimore City/Montgomery/PG County hit 8.95% combined income tax.
taxes.contractor_tax: Contractor as consumer of materials (standard model).

notes_2026: Berenter doctrine: unlicensed HIC contract = VOID entirely. Homeowner keeps work AND recovers deposits. Among the harshest unlicensed-contractor penalties in the US.
Judicial-lien procedure (vs recorded lien) is unique to Maryland among states audited.
Pre-work lien waivers are VOID as against public policy (§ 9-113(b)).
Deposit cap of 33⅓% of contract price on home improvement contracts (Bus. Reg. § 8-501(c)).
MHIC Guaranty Fund pays consumers $30K/claim, $200K aggregate per contractor; Fund payment triggers immediate license suspension until reimbursed.
Prompt Pay interest is 18%/yr (1.5%/month) — among the highest in the country.
Workplace Fraud Act ABC test (Lab. & Empl. § 3-903) — most subs fail prong C; stop-work orders + treble back wages.
No statewide commercial GC license — county/city patchwork.

licensing.board: Mississippi State Board of Contractors
requirements: Commercial license required for projects over $50,000
Residential license required for projects over $10,000
Pass Mississippi Law and Business Management Exam
Surety bond required
Insurance required
